fund.name: Nippon India Multi Cap Fund - Direct Plan Growth Plan - Growth Option
fund.fund_house: Nippon Life India Asset Management Limited

allocations.rationale: Nippon India Multi Cap Fund is a well-diversified equity fund with a moderate risk profile. It's suitable for long-term goals like retirement and children's education. It has a good track record with consistent returns over the last 5 years.

fund.name: UTI - Credit Risk Fund (Segregated - 17022020) - Direct Plan - Growth Option

allocations.rationale: UTI Credit Risk Fund is a debt fund with a low risk profile. It's suitable for short-term goals like buying a house. It has a good track record with consistent returns over the last year.

plan.setup_phase: Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Groww, Kuvera, or MFU. Step 2: Start your monthly SIPs in these funds. Step 3: Set up a reminder to review your portfolio once a year.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: check if your SIPs are running. After 1 year: see if any fund has fallen behind compared to similar funds.
After 3 years: review your portfolio and see if any fund has grown much more than planned. If so, move some money to the others to get back to your target mix.
After 5 years: review your portfolio and see if any fund has grown much more than planned. If so, move some money to the others to get back to your target mix.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year, check if your mix has changed a lot. If one category has grown much more than planned, move some money to the others to get back to your target mix.
plan.rationale: Hermione's investment plan is designed to balance growth and safety. The majority of her investments are in equity funds to grow her corpus over the long term, while a portion is in debt funds to provide stability. This plan is suitable for her moderate risk appetite and long-term investment horizon.
risks: Stock markets can drop 20-30% in a bad year — your portfolio value will temporarily go down. This is normal and recovers over time.
Debt funds are safer but offer lower returns. They are suitable for short-term goals like buying a house.
The plan is designed to balance growth and safety. It's suitable for a moderate risk appetite and long-term investment horizon.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth

aps.reasoning: The plan allocates 70% to equity funds, but all are actively managed Multi Cap funds with high alpha and tracking error, indicating active management. The turnover score is 0.5 because the plan recommends annual rebalancing, which is less frequent than monthly or quarterly but more than once every few years. Cost emphasis is moderate as expense ratios are mentioned but not as a primary selection criterion. The plan does not emphasize research, relying instead on fund track records. The time horizon is long-term, aligning with the passive score. The portfolio is active due to the high alpha and tracking error of the funds.

pqs.reasoning: Goal alignment is good but not perfect. The plan focuses on long-term goals like retirement and children's education with equity allocation, but doesn't address the short-term goal of buying a house adequately. Diversification is poor as the plan heavily concentrates in a single fund (Nippon India Multi Cap Fund) across multiple variants, which violates the investor's preference to avoid sectoral concentration. Risk return appropriateness is high as the moderate risk appetite is matched with a balanced portfolio. Internal consistency is acceptable as the rationale and allocations align, though the repeated use of the same fund is a minor inconsistency.
